pattern: Converting each date string to a day-count from a fixed epoch and subtracting removes all calendar complexity from the main logic.
hints: You need to map every date to a single integer representing its position in time — days elapsed since some fixed reference point.
Building that mapping requires handling leap years (divisible by 4, except centuries unless also divisible by 400) and the varying month lengths.
Convert both dates to their day-count from a reference epoch, then return the absolute difference.

edge_inputs.description: dates spanning a year boundary, second earlier than first
spanning a leap day (Feb 29 exists in 2000)
2100 is not a leap year (century not divisible by 400)
year 2000 is a leap year (divisible by 400) — Feb has 29 days

stress.description: random pairs of dates within the valid range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    def rand_date(rng):
        year = rng.randint(1971, 2100)
        month = rng.randint(1, 12)
        def is_leap(y): return (y % 4 == 0 and y % 100 != 0) or (y % 400 == 0)
        days_in = [0,31,29 if is_leap(year) else 28,31,30,31,30,31,31,30,31,30,31]
        day = rng.randint(1, days_in[month])
        return f'{year:04d}-{month:02d}-{day:02d}'
    d1 = rand_date(rng)
    d2 = rand_date(rng)
    return [d1, d2]
